root_text.an5.82:1.1: “Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.82:1.3: Avītarāgo hoti, avītadoso hoti, avītamoho hoti, makkhī ca, paḷāsī ca—
root_text.an5.82:1.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ appiyo ca hoti amanāpo ca agaru ca abhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.82:2.1: Pañcahi, bhikkhave, dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo ca.
root_text.an5.82:2.3: Vītarāgo hoti, vītadoso hoti, vītamoho hoti, amakkhī ca, apaḷāsī ca—
root_text.an5.82:2.4: imehi kho, bhikkhave, pañcahi dhammehi samannāgato thero bhikkhu sabrahmacārīnaṁ piyo ca hoti manāpo ca garu ca bhāvanīyo cā”ti.

translation_text.an5.82:1.1: “Mendicants, a senior mendicant with five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.82:1.3: They’re not free of greed, hate, and delusion; they are offensive and contemptuous.
translation_text.an5.82:1.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is unlikable and disagreeable to their spiritual companions, not respected or admired.
translation_text.an5.82:2.1: A senior mendicant with five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.
translation_text.an5.82:2.3: They’re free of greed, hate, and delusion; they’re not offensive and contemptuous.
translation_text.an5.82:2.4: A senior mendicant with these five qualities is dear and beloved to their spiritual companions, respected and admired.”
